Package org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise

Class DescribeQuickActionListItemResult

java.lang.Object
org.solenopsis.soap.enterprise.DescribeQuickActionListItemResult
public class DescribeQuickActionListItemResult extends Object

Java class for DescribeQuickActionListItemResult complex type

.

The following schema fragment specifies the expected content contained within this class.


 <complexType name="DescribeQuickActionListItemResult">
   <complexContent>
     <restriction base="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}anyType">
       <sequence>
         <element name="accessLevelRequired" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}ShareAccessLevel"/>
         <element name="colors" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}DescribeColor" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="iconUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="icons" type="{urn:enterprise.soap.sforce.com}DescribeIcon" maxOccurs="unbounded" minOccurs="0"/>
         <element name="label" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="miniIconUrl" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="quickActionName" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="targetSobjectType" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
         <element name="type" type="{http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema}string"/>
       </sequence>
     </restriction>
   </complexContent>
 </complexType>

    • getAccessLevelRequired

      public ShareAccessLevel getAccessLevelRequired()

      Returns:
      possible object is ShareAccessLevel

    • setAccessLevelRequired

      public void setAccessLevelRequired(ShareAccessLevel value)
      Sets the value of the accessLevelRequired property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is ShareAccessLevel

    • getColors

      public List<DescribeColor> getColors()
      Gets the value of the colors property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the colors property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getColors().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list DescribeColor

      Returns:
      The value of the colors property.

    • getIconUrl

      public String getIconUrl()
      Gets the value of the iconUrl property.
      Returns:
      possible object is String

      Sets the value of the iconUrl property.
      Parameters:
      value - allowed object is String

    • getIcons

      public List<DescribeIcon> getIcons()
      Gets the value of the icons property.

      This accessor method returns a reference to the live list, not a snapshot. Therefore, any modification you make to the returned list will be present inside the Jakarta XML Binding object. This is why there is not a set method for the icons property.

      For example, to add a new item, do as follows: 

       getIcons().add(newItem);

      Objects of the following type(s) are allowed in the list DescribeIcon

      Returns:
      The value of the icons property.
